channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google ordered to open up Play app store to competition
item.description: A U.S. judge on Monday ordered Alphabet’s Google to overhaul its mobile app business to give Android users more options to download apps and to pay for transactions within them, following a jury verdict last year for Fortnite maker Epic Games. The injunction by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: American Water Works cyberattack: Water supplier says its systems were hacked
item.description: The largest supplier of drinking water and wastewater services in the U.S. is the latest target to be hit by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: H-1B visa: Company supplying thousands of tech workers to Silicon Valley discriminated against non-Indians, jury finds
item.description: The H-1B has become a political flashpoint in the U.S. Critics point to abuses including replacement of U.S. workers by visa holders, while the tech industry lobbies to boost the annual cap on new visas past 85,000.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: This tiny super cheap $13 PC is barely bigger than an adult pinkie finger and has no OS — and yet this hacker-friendly device packs a display, MicroSD card slot plus Wi-Fi connectivity
item.description: Waveshare’s ESP32-S3-LCD-1.47 compact USB dongle packs a display, MicroSD card slot, and Wi-Fi connectivity
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: American Water, largest water utility in US, dealing with cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Control the path and power of hurricanes like Milton? Forget it, scientists say
item.description: In the wake of hurricanes that have disrupted lives for millions in the U.S., some people may wonder if extreme weather events could be somehow controlled.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling And Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rust resumes rise in popularity
item.description: Rust, the fast and memory-safe programming language born out of Mozilla, has resumed its rise up the monthly Tiobe index of programming language popularity, although it still has not cracked the index’s top 10.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Helene conspiracy theories on social media are harming disaster relief efforts
item.description: In the wake of the devastation of Hurricane Helene in the United States this week, a new storm emerged on social media — false rumors about how disaster funds have been used, and even claims that officials control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: EU ruling clamps down on Meta’s use of personal data for ads
item.description: The European Court of Justice has decided that Facebook owner Meta must minimize the amount of personal data it uses for personalized ads, the BBC reports . The decision from the EU’s top court means that only a small part of Meta’s data collection can be used for advertisements.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This new AI answer engine plans to pay media companies for their content
item.description: When ChatGPT launched two years ago, it blew open the doors to a new way to search for information online. Instead of sifting through a search engine’s list of links, users can now query a chatbot and get an actual answer.
item.dc:creator: David Salazar, Fast Company

item.title: French government leaves $1.1 billion hole in Atos refinancing plan, Atos says don’t worry
item.description: Atos has failed to agree terms with the French government for the sale of part of its business, one of several elements of its plan to refinance its debt.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Controversy Surrounds Mt. Diablo Unified School District's Energy Contract - $50 Million Deal Sparks Accusations of Lax Oversight, Bloated Costs
item.description: Concerns arise over nearly $50 million energy contract between Mt. Diablo Unified School District and Schneider Electric, with accusations of lax oversight, bloated costs, and failure to deliver on promised savings.
item.dc:creator: Katie Lauer, The Mercury News

item.title: How Charlie Health wants to tackle the loneliness epidemic
item.description: Loneliness is reaching epidemic levels, driving up cases of anxiety, depression, and contributing to record-high suicide rates. Carter Barnhart, founder of the virtual mental health platform Charlie Health, says the issue goes beyond simple explanations like social media overuse.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Ask Kurt: How does Apple AirTag work, how far can you track?
item.description: Can you use an AirTag to track a pet? Is it worth it? Here’s how far it will track. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Using your Apple Watch to monitor blood pressure
item.description: Checking your blood pressure has become simple with the Apple Watch, and doing this can help you identify trends and manage medications.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Understanding exit codes on Linux
item.description: Whenever you run a command in a Linux terminal, a numeric exit code is generated – even if you see no sign of it. The code reports the success or failure of the command. And, if the command fails, it provides a value that hints at the problem. If the exit code is 0, all is well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
